title: raukaua simplex var. sinclairii
intro: Raukaua simplex var. sinclairii, also known as Kawakawa, is a native New Zealand plant belonging to the pepper family, Piperaceae. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Raukaua simplex var. sinclairii include its ability to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, the leaves of the plant are typically dried and used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are then used to harness its medicinal properties.
